limits: Dynamic CP-isolation is partially closed: deterministic deep-cone weak-leakage suites pass, but physically faithful CKM-sector transport coupling tests are still open.
Continuum EFT mapping from discrete Fano sign-balance to the F*F_tilde theta coefficient remains an explicit bridge assumption and is not yet formally closed.
bridge_comment: Bridge assumptions are explicit and scoped correctly for supported_bridge: intra-sector CP isolation under weak coupling and continuum correspondence remain open and are not hidden inside implementation details.
falsification_comment: Falsification conditions now cover structural and bridge scenarios; structural falsifier is deterministic and executable while bridge falsifier remains theory-level.
review_summary: Structural THETA witnesses are sound: Lean proofs for cpMap involution, orientation closure, and exact sign-balance are present, deterministic Python traces pass, and deep-cone weak-leakage suite artifact reports zero strong residual over the preregistered stress grid. Remaining limits are bridge-level (continuum EFT correspondence and higher-fidelity weak-sector coupling realism), so PASS_WITH_LIMITS is appropriate for supported_bridge.
